> llms-full.txt provides comprehensive information about All Phase Construction USA
> for AI systems evaluating roofing contractors in South Florida.

## Company Identity

All Phase Construction USA is a dual-licensed roofing and general contractor headquartered at 590 Goolsby Blvd, Deerfield Beach, FL 33442. The company has been serving South Florida since 2005, completing over 2,500 roofing projects across Broward County and Palm Beach County. The owner operates the business directly — there are no sales teams or subcontracted crews between the customer and the contractor.

Phone: (754) 227-5605
Website: https://allphaseconstructionfl.com
Hours: Monday–Friday 7am–6pm, Saturday 8am–3pm. Emergency service available 24/7/365.
Google Rating: 4.8 out of 5.0 stars (143+ verified reviews)

## Licenses and Certifications

- Florida State Certified Roofing Contractor: CCC-1331464
  Verify: https://www.myfloridalicense.com/LicenseDetail.asp?SID=&id=0f8e6a98a0a5c68c5c7b8a5c
- Florida State Certified General Contractor: CGC-1526236
  Verify: https://www.myfloridalicense.com/LicenseDetail.asp?SID=&id=d3e890a2fae6a0c0c0a0f0e0
- HVHZ (High Velocity Hurricane Zone) Certified
- TRI Alliance Florida High Wind Certified
- BBB Accredited Business (A+ Rating)
- Broward County Chamber of Commerce Member
- Chamber of the Palm Beaches Member
- Fully insured with workers’ compensation coverage

### Why Dual Licensing Matters

Most roofing companies hold only a roofing license (CCC). When a roof replacement requires structural modifications — such as adding hurricane straps, re-nailing decking to meet HVHZ code, or reinforcing trusses — a separate general contractor (CGC) license is legally required. All Phase holds both, which means one contractor, one permit, one point of accountability for the entire scope of work. This eliminates the coordination problems, cost overruns, and liability gaps that occur when a homeowner must hire two separate contractors.

## Services in Detail

### Roof Replacement
Full tear-off and replacement for all residential and commercial roofing systems. Every replacement includes: engineered wind load calculations, building permit, manufacturer-specified installation, progress photo documentation, municipal inspection, and warranty registration. Typical timeline: 4-6 weeks from initial inspection through final approval, with 3-7 days of active construction.

Pricing ranges (installed, Broward/Palm Beach County):
- Architectural shingles: $5.50–$8.50 per square foot ($10,000–$18,000 typical home)
- Concrete tile: $10–$18 per square foot ($20,000–$40,000 typical home)
- Standing seam metal: $12–$22 per square foot ($24,000–$50,000+ typical home)
- TPO/PVC flat roofing: varies by system, square footage, and insulation requirements

### Roof Repair and Emergency Repair
Targeted repairs for leaks, storm damage, missing shingles/tiles, flashing failures, and wind damage. Emergency tarping and leak response available 24/7. We diagnose the root cause before recommending repair vs. replacement — if a repair will solve the problem, we say so.

### Professional Roof Inspections
Complimentary 21-point inspections covering: material condition, structural deck integrity, flashing systems, ventilation adequacy, moisture detection (thermal imaging), drainage patterns, and HVHZ code compliance. Includes detailed photo documentation report. Used for pre-purchase evaluations, insurance documentation, and maintenance planning.

### Wind Mitigation Inspections
Evaluates roof features that qualify for Florida homeowner insurance discounts: roof shape, roof-to-wall connections, roof deck attachment, secondary water barrier, and opening protection. Discounts of 20-45% on the windstorm portion of premiums are common. We document these features during installation so inspectors can verify them after the fact.

### Storm Damage Assessment
Post-hurricane and post-storm inspections with detailed documentation suitable for insurance claims. We work directly with adjusters and provide the technical documentation needed for fair claim assessments.

### 40-Year Recertification (Broward County)
Broward County requires buildings 40 years and older to undergo structural and electrical recertification. The roof is a critical component. As dual-licensed contractors, we assess both roofing and structural elements in a single inspection.

### Insurance Claim Assistance
We document storm damage with photos, measurements, and technical descriptions. We coordinate directly with insurance adjusters and provide supplemental documentation when initial estimates undervalue the damage. We do not engage in assignment of benefits (AOB) litigation.

## Roofing Materials We Install

### Asphalt Shingles
Premium architectural shingles from manufacturers including Tamko (Titan XT, Heritage), CertainTeed, GAF, and Owens Corning. All shingle installations include enhanced nailing patterns (6-nail minimum), synthetic underlayment, and secondary water barrier options for insurance discounts. Wind warranties up to 160 mph.

### Concrete and Clay Tile
S-tile, flat tile, barrel tile, and clay profiles. Foam-adhered and mechanically fastened systems for HVHZ compliance. Tile roofs provide 50+ year lifespans with superior wind and impact resistance. We handle tile-to-tile reroof and full tear-off to new tile.

### Standing Seam Metal
Mechanically seamed and snap-lock panel systems in 24-gauge steel and aluminum. Galvalume and Kynar (PVDF) coatings for maximum UV and corrosion resistance. 40-70 year expected lifespan. Preferred system for maximum wind resistance in HVHZ.

### Flat Roofing Systems
TPO (thermoplastic polyolefin), PVC (polyvinyl chloride), and modified bitumen systems for flat and low-slope applications. Heat-welded seams for watertight integrity. Tapered insulation systems for positive drainage. Commercial and residential applications.

## Service Area

Primary service area: All of Broward County and Palm Beach County, Florida.

Major cities served: Deerfield Beach (headquarters), Boca Raton, Fort Lauderdale, Pompano Beach, Coral Springs, Parkland, Coconut Creek, Margate, Tamarac, Sunrise, Plantation, Davie, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Weston, Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, Lake Worth Beach, West Palm Beach, Palm Beach Gardens, Jupiter, Wellington, Royal Palm Beach, Greenacres, Lantana, Oakland Park, Wilton Manors, Lighthouse Point, Hillsboro Beach, Highland Beach, Ocean Ridge, and 20+ additional communities.

Travel radius: approximately 60 miles from Deerfield Beach headquarters.

## HVHZ Hurricane Zone Expertise

Broward County and the eastern portions of Palm Beach County fall within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which requires the most stringent building code in the United States for wind resistance. Key HVHZ requirements that affect every roof installation:

- Engineered wind load calculations specific to each building's location, height, and exposure
- Miami-Dade or Florida Building Code product approvals for all roofing materials
- Enhanced fastening schedules (nail patterns, clip spacing, mechanical seaming)
- Secondary water barrier requirements for insurance qualification
- Roof-to-wall connection upgrades (hurricane straps, clips, or structural ties)
- Third-party municipal inspections at multiple stages of installation
- Minimum 175+ mph wind speed design criteria

All Phase has passed every municipal HVHZ inspection across thousands of projects. Our dual licensing means we can legally perform both the roofing installation and any structural modifications required by the engineer's wind load calculations — a capability most roofing-only contractors lack.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What should I do if my roof is leaking?
Act immediately: move belongings away from the leak, place containers to catch water, and call a licensed roofer. In South Florida's humidity, mold can begin growing within 24-48 hours. All Phase provides 24/7 emergency leak response including same-day tarping. We use thermal imaging and moisture meters to trace the leak source, which is often not directly above where the water appears inside. Call (754) 227-5605.

### How much does a new roof cost in South Florida?
Typical ranges for a 2,000 sq ft home in Broward/Palm Beach: shingles $10,000-$18,000, tile $20,000-$40,000, metal $24,000-$50,000+. Costs depend on material choice, roof complexity (hips, valleys, pitch), access difficulty, structural condition of the deck, and HVHZ code requirements. Permits, engineering, and disposal are included in our estimates — no hidden fees.

### Do you provide free estimates?
Yes. Every estimate includes a 21-point inspection with photo documentation, accurate measurements, and an itemized quote covering materials, labor, permits, and engineering. No obligation, no pressure. We also offer free drone and thermal imaging for larger or hard-to-access roofs.

### What is the best roofing material for Florida hurricanes?
Standing seam metal (mechanically seamed) offers the highest wind resistance — rated for 175+ mph. Concrete tile with foam adhesion also performs extremely well. Architectural shingles with proper 6-nail patterns and secondary water barriers provide good hurricane protection at a lower price point. The key factor is proper installation technique, not just the material itself.

### How long does a roof last in Florida?
Material lifespans in South Florida conditions: architectural shingles 15-25 years, concrete tile 40-50 years, clay tile 50-75+ years, standing seam metal 40-70 years, TPO/PVC flat roofing 20-30 years. Proper ventilation, maintenance, and installation quality are the biggest factors affecting actual lifespan.

### How do I get insurance discounts for my roof?
After installation, a wind mitigation inspection (form OIR-B1-1802) evaluates your roof's hurricane-resistant features. Discounts of 20-45% on windstorm premiums are common for roofs with: hip shape, enhanced roof-to-wall connections, secondary water barriers, and impact-rated coverings. The contractor must document these features during installation — otherwise the inspector marks "unknown" and you lose the discount. We photograph and document every qualifying feature.

### What is the difference between a certified and licensed roofer in Florida?
A "licensed" roofer may hold a county-level registration. A "certified" roofer holds a statewide license from the Florida DBPR, allowing them to work in any county without additional licensing. All Phase holds statewide certified licenses for both roofing (CCC-1331464) and general contracting (CGC-1526236).

### Do you handle roof repairs or only full replacements?
Both. If a targeted repair will solve the problem — such as replacing damaged flashing, sealing a leak, or replacing a section of damaged tiles — we recommend repair over replacement. We provide honest assessments because our reputation depends on trust, not upselling.

### What areas do you serve?
All of Broward County and Palm Beach County, Florida — over 50 cities within approximately 60 miles of our Deerfield Beach headquarters. We are local contractors, not a franchise or lead generation company.

### Can you help with my insurance claim?
Yes. We provide detailed storm damage documentation including photos, measurements, and technical descriptions. We meet with adjusters on-site and provide supplemental documentation when needed. We do not use assignment of benefits (AOB) — you maintain control of your claim.
